rulings.Case Title: GAVIN G. HANLIN ET AL VS. SOJOURN PROPERTIES INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 4, 2024 line 11. PLAINTIFF GAVIN HANLIN, CHAYTON HANLIN DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER TO Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. 53rd affirmative defense is sufficiently stated and is not uncertain as to the terms of the purported release. The existence/enforceability of such purported release is not an issue properly addressed on a demurrer. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC VS. TOLTEC CAPITAL LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Requests For Admissions Propounded On David Cohen, Set One, Be Deemed Admitted And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $770.00; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Herein
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 4, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFF RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER THAT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS PROPOUNDED ON DAVID COHEN, SET ONE, BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $770.00; AND MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES HEREIN is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and establish that Defendant failed to timely respond to the Request for Admissions. Declaration of Paul Belva is not dated and reference to the timing of the failure to respond in paragraph 4 of the declaration is "to date." =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC VS. TOLTEC CAPITAL LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Requests For Admissions Propounded On Pablo Gomez, Set One, Be Deemed Admitted And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $770.00; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Herein
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 4, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFF RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER THAT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS PROPOUNDED ON PABLO GOMEZ, SET ONE, BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $770.00; AND MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES HEREIN is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and establish that Defendant failed to timely respond to the Request for Admissions. Declaration of Paul Belva is not dated and reference to the timing of the failure to respond in paragraph 4 of the declaration is "to date." Additionally, this motion appears to be moot in light of the opposition containing purported responses to the Request for Admissions. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
